text.title: Design System Success Factors Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the design-system-success-factors methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Every design system MUST have a single named accountable owner; split ownership without RACI is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Distributed ownership produces decision paralysis; single owner with explicit responsibility model is what works.

rule.statement: Documentation MUST be generated from source (Storybook stories + JSDoc) — hand-written docs that drift are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Docs/code drift is the most common system-rot signal; generated docs cannot drift.

rule.statement: Adoption MUST be tracked per consuming team (% imports from system vs alternatives); unmeasured adoption is unmanaged.
rule.rationale: Without per-team metrics, leadership cannot identify which teams need support.

rule.statement: Contribution model (proposal RFC → review → merge) MUST be documented + linked from every component README; ad-hoc PRs without RFC MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Without explicit model, contributors give up or fork; explicit RFC unblocks community contribution.

rule.statement: Coverage (system components / total product surface components) MUST be tracked quarterly with a stated target; <70% triggers a remediation plan.
rule.rationale: Low coverage means teams ship custom — the system becomes irrelevant.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
